title: Manet's Olympia
description: This quiz is for the video Manet, Olympia.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Olympia’s confrontation with the viewer highlights her as a [[☃ dropdown 1]], asserts her status as a [[☃ dropdown 2]], and forces the viewer to acknowledge their motivations and desires.**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/86cb52b72bd3afa44f415d2c027d7041.png)
hints: **Olympia’s confrontation with the viewer highlights her as a real woman, asserts her status as a prostitute, and forces the viewer to acknowledge their motivations and desires.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Manet modernized the tradition of the female nude by rejecting academic conventions of . . .** (choose two)


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/86cb52b72bd3afa44f415d2c027d7041.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **Manet modernized the tradition of the female nude by rejecting Academic conventions of chiaroscuro (modeling) in the figure’s form and by directly engaing the viewer.**

choices.content: the horizontal composition of the image of the female nude
color for the background of the image
the relationship of the nude to the viewer
chiaroscuro, or modeling, in the figure's form

assessment_items.question_content:
**Manet's *Olympia* answered Baudelaire's call to represent the beauty of modern life by . . .**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/86cb52b72bd3afa44f415d2c027d7041.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **Manet's *Olympia* answers Baudelaire's call to represent the beauty of modern life by presenting an alternative to the idealized beauty of the past.**

choices.content: emphasizing classical idealism in the nude figure.
drawing attention to issues of class and race.
showing the luxury of 19th century life.
presenting a modern alternative to the idealized beauty of the past.

assessment_items.question_content:
**The tradition of the female nude refers to the erotic and sensual representation of women, disguised in classical mythology and ideals of beauty.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **The tradition of the female nude refers to the erotic and sensual representation of naked women, disguised in classical mythology and ideals of beauty (for example Titian's *Venus of Urbino*.**
